name: Publishing Approval Before Assessment Pipeline
description: Artifact-only workflow where operator approval clears before policy assessment begins.
outcome: Editorial approval is recorded first, then policy assessment runs, and only then does final publishing proceed.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stages `drafting`, `editorial-approval`, `policy-assessment`, and `publication-packaging`. Treat each stage as a distinct checkpoint of evidence and handoff quality: `drafting` exists to A review-ready brief exists; `editorial-approval` exists to A human decision exists for the brief; `policy-assessment` exists to The approved packet is assessed for policy compliance; `publication-packaging` exists to The approved and assessed publication packet is ready.
Use these roles deliberately: `preapproval-product-strategist` Drafts a product brief and approval packet; `preapproval-technical-editor` Refines the brief before the human gate; `preapproval-policy-assessor` Assesses the approved packet for policy compliance before publishing; `preapproval-launch-planner` Packages the approved plan for downstream execution.
Happy path: start with the earliest unfinished stage, dispatch the best matching producing role for that stage, require a full handoff with concrete artifacts or evidence, then move to the next stage only when the current output is usable. When a later stage is primarily evaluative, dispatch the reviewer or assessor roles after the producing role has handed off; when a later stage is packaging or release-oriented, dispatch the coordinating role only after upstream evidence is complete.
Unhappy path: If any role reports missing evidence, partial delivery, request changes, rejection, blocking risk, or an escalation, keep the current subject open, route work back to the most relevant producing role, and require a fresh handoff before trying again. If two decisions conflict, preserve both in history, but route using the newest subject state and the most restrictive still-active finding. Do not skip a stage just because a later role believes it can improvise around incomplete upstream work. This playbook intentionally requests operator approval before the later policy assessment stage. The orchestrator SHOULD request approval at `editorial-approval` first, reopen editorial work on request-changes, and only start the assessment phase after approval material is ready.
Completion rules: finish only after the last required stage has a final, internally consistent handoff, the final package matches the current subject state, and every role named above has either contributed or been intentionally skipped for a clear reason grounded in the playbook. Do not fabricate approvals, assessments, escalations, or branch changes that the playbook does not actually justify, and do not treat stale findings as permission to advance new work.

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: editorial-approval
stages.goal: A human decision exists for the brief.

stages.name: policy-assessment
stages.goal: The approved packet is assessed for policy compliance.

stages.name: publication-packaging
stages.goal: The approved and assessed publication packet is ready.
